source_jobs.title: Coordinator, Digital Marketing & Commercial Partnerships
source_jobs.description:
About The Company

Reservoir is an independent music company based in New York City and with offices in Los Angeles, Nashville, Toronto, London, Abu Dhabi, and Mumbai. Reservoir is the first female-founded and led publicly traded independent music company in the U.S. Founded as a family-owned music publisher in 2007, Reservoir has grown to represent over 150,000 copyrights and 36,000 master recordings with titles dating as far back as 1900 and hundreds of #1 releases worldwide. Reservoir frequently holds a Top 10 U.S. Market Share according to Billboard’s Publishers Quarterly, was twice named Publisher of the Year by Music Business Worldwide’s The A&R Awards and won Independent Publisher of the Year at the 2020 and 2022 Music Week Awards.


Reservoir also represents a multitude of recorded music through Chrysalis Records, Tommy Boy Records, and Philly Groove Records and manages artists through its ventures with Blue Raincoat Music and Big Life Management.

The Position

The Coordinator, Digital Marketing & Commercial Partnerships, will support Reservoir’s US Recorded Music marketing team across catalog and frontline campaigns. Reporting to the Director of Digital Marketing & Commercial Partnerships, this role will help drive streaming growth, fan engagement, and audience development through DSP partnerships, commercial pitching, digital marketing, influencer campaigns, and direct-to-fan initiatives. The role includes, but is not limited to, the following responsibilities.

 - Manage organization and population of release pitching details and assets in Airtable, including feature.fm links, descriptions, marketing/pitch summaries, and artist photos.

 - Pitching releases in DSP forms and backends across US-based projects.

 - Share weekly updates on external releases and priorities with external DSP stakeholders.

 - Develop relationships with DSP and editorial contacts on behalf of the company/clients.

 - Administer all US label-credit advertising campaigns, including TikTok and Spotify ads, to support the growth strategy on these platforms.

 - Support a label-owned playlist ecosystem to drive streaming growth, including creating new playlisting strategies and implementation.

 - Support all digital marketing strategies to drive revenue growth, including paid media, creator marketing, content marketing, D2F marketing, and viral marketing techniques and strategies.

 - Create and edit content as-needed for marketing campaigns and owned media assets, such as advertising banners/videos, social media cutdowns, and newsletters.

 - Weekly streaming summaries and digital performance reports, including playlisting wrap-ups, advertising reports, and bespoke marketing campaign reports.

 - Act as the first point of contact for the go-to-market best practices for partner labels.

 - Support quarterly planning across catalog, frontline, and anniversary projects.

Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s degree required.

 - Experience in music marketing is a plus, but not required.

 - Familiarity with CRM, project management, and email marketing platforms (e.g., Airtable, HubSpot, Mailchimp, or similar).

 - Proficiency with content creation and editing tools, including Canva and Adobe Creative Suite.

 - Strong understanding of social media marketing, fan engagement, and audience growth across platforms such as TikTok, YouTube, Reddit, and Snapchat.

 - Working knowledge of digital advertising fundamentals and marketing technology platforms.

 - Proficiency in Microsoft Office and Google Workspace.

 - Excellent communication, relationship-building, and stakeholder management skills.

 - Highly organized, self-motivated, and able to manage multiple priorities while meeting deadlines.

 - Quick learner with the initiative to take ownership and continuously develop expertise.

 - Passion for music, fan culture, and the evolving digital music ecosystem, including streaming and artist-to-fan engagement.

Equal Employment Opportunity

source_jobs.title: Manager, Synch Licensing
source_jobs.description:
About The Company

Reservoir is an independent music company based in New York City and with offices in Los Angeles, Nashville, Toronto, London, Abu Dhabi, and Mumbai. Reservoir is the first female-founded and led publicly traded independent music company in the U.S. Founded as a family-owned music publisher in 2007, Reservoir has grown to represent over 150,000 copyrights and 36,000 master recordings with titles dating as far back as 1900 and hundreds of #1 releases worldwide. Reservoir frequently holds a Top 10 U.S. Market Share according to Billboard’s Publishers Quarterly, was twice named Publisher of the Year by Music Business Worldwide’s The A&R Awards and won Independent Publisher of the Year at the 2020 and 2022 Music Week Awards.


Reservoir also represents a multitude of recorded music through Chrysalis Records, Tommy Boy Records, and Philly Groove Records and manages artists through its ventures with Blue Raincoat Music and Big Life Management.

The Position

The Manager, Synch Licensing supports the day-to-day operations of Reservoir's synchronization licensing business. Working closely with the Licensing, Clearance, and Royalty teams, this role is responsible for preparing agreements and invoices, tracking licenses and payments, maintaining accurate records, and helping ensure deals move efficiently from approval through payment collection. The role includes, but is not limited to, the following responsibilities.

 - Prepare and issue synchronization license agreements, invoices, and related licensing documentation.

 - Maintain accurate digital records for all licensing projects, including correspondence, agreements, invoices, payments, and supporting documentation.

 - Partner with the Royalty department to facilitate the processing and tracking of incoming synchronization payments.

 - Maintain and update licensing databases to accurately reflect the status of licenses, invoices, and payments.

 - Collaborate with the Clearance team to ensure deal terms and licensing information are complete and accurate.

 - Distribute invoices, W-9 forms, vendor documentation, and other required materials to licensees and clearance companies.

 - Monitor outstanding receivables, follow up on license fee collections, process incoming payments, and reconcile payment activity with the Royalty department.

 - Review daily payment notifications to identify and allocate incoming synchronization revenue.

 - Assist with monthly payment reconciliations and reporting activities.

 - Support process improvements and other departmental initiatives as needed.

Qualifications

 - Bachelor's degree in Music Business, Business Administration, Accounting, Finance, or a related field.

 - 5+ years of experience in music licensing, synchronization licensing, copyright administration, or a related music industry role.

 - Strong understanding of music licensing, synchronization, copyright, and music clearance processes.

 - Experience preparing licensing agreements, invoices, and managing payment workflows.

 - Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including PivotTables, XLOOKUP/VLOOKUP, and formulas; proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite.

 - Exceptional organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines in a fast-paced environment.

 - Strong analytical skills with excellent attention to detail and accuracy.

 - Ability to handle confidential and sensitive information with professionalism and discretion.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

 - Self-motivated with a proactive approach to problem-solving and continuous process improvement.

Equal Employment Opportunity

Reservoir is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. We consider all qualified applicants without regard to age, race, ethnicity, color, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, marital status, military status, or any other character

source_jobs.title: Coordinator, Licensing & Copyright
source_jobs.description:
About The Company

Reservoir is an independent music company based in New York City and with offices in Los Angeles, Nashville, Toronto, London, Abu Dhabi, and Mumbai. Reservoir is the first female-founded and led publicly traded independent music company in the U.S. Founded as a family-owned music publisher in 2007, Reservoir has grown to represent over 150,000 copyrights and 36,000 master recordings with titles dating as far back as 1900 and hundreds of #1 releases worldwide. Reservoir frequently holds a Top 10 U.S. Market Share according to Billboard’s Publishers Quarterly, was twice named Publisher of the Year by Music Business Worldwide’s The A&R Awards and won Independent Publisher of the Year at the 2020 and 2022 Music Week Awards.


Reservoir also represents a multitude of recorded music through Chrysalis Records, Tommy Boy Records, and Philly Groove Records and manages artists through its ventures with Blue Raincoat Music and Big Life Management.

The Position

The Coordinator, Licensing & Copyright plays a key role in maintaining the accuracy and integrity of Reservoir's publishing catalog across internal systems and external platforms. This position partners closely with the Licensing & Copyright team and cross-functional departments to support catalog administration, resolve rights-related issues, and help improve data quality, workflows, and operational processes.

 - Support the onboarding of new songs, clients, and related assets into Reservoir's internal databases.

 - Research and resolve missing or incomplete catalog metadata for new and existing agreements.

 - Assist in managing Reservoir's catalog across digital platforms, including YouTube, TikTok, Meta, and other rights management systems by submitting claims and resolving conflicts.

 - Investigate and resolve conflicts identified by Reservoir's sub-publishing network.

 - Confirm publishing ownership splits with record labels, performing rights organizations, and other industry partners.

 - Research catalog issues across direct sources to identify and resolve problems affecting content availability and playability.

 - Process, compile, and respond to ACK files while ensuring accurate catalog ingestion.

 - Participate in departmental initiatives, process improvements, and other special projects as assigned.

Qualifications

 - Bachelor's degree in Music Business, Business Administration, or a related field.

 - 1–2 years of experience in music publishing, rights administration, copyright, or a related field.

 - Working knowledge of music publishing agreements, copyright administration, and royalty structures.

 - Experience with Music Maestro or similar music rights administration systems.

 - Familiarity with ASCAP, BMI, The MLC, and other collection society portals.

 - Experience with digital rights management platforms, including YouTube CMS, Meta Rights Manager, TikTok, or similar systems.

 - Advanced Microsoft Excel skills, including VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, PivotTables, and formulas.

 - Strong analytical skills with exceptional attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

 - A passion for music, publishing, and technology.

Equal Employment Opportunity

Reservoir is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. We consider all qualified applicants without regard to age, race, ethnicity, color, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, marital status, military status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We believe that embracing diversity strengthens our culture, drives innovation, and contributes to our continued success.

If you require assistance or an accommodation in completing any aspect of the application process, please contact the Reservoir Recruiting team at: jobs@reservoir-media.com.
